text.content:
📔 Vel'Nathis — GM Guide

Module: RuneForge Studio — Vel'Nathis & The Shard
Contents: 1 Actor (Vel'Nathis, Tiny Fiend, CR 4) • 1 Item (Shard of Vel'Nathis, Very Rare Loot) • 1 RollTable (Compulsion Table, 1d10) • This Journal
Tier: 2–3 (player levels 5–10)
Tone: Psychological horror, slow corruption, long-game manipulation

⚠️ How to Use This Module

This module is a self-contained story engine, not a single encounter. Vel'Nathis is designed to operate across multiple sessions — a villain who never appears on the battlefield, never threatens directly, and wins by making the party slowly distrust each other.

To deploy: drop the Shard of Vel'Nathis item into the world as treasure. Once a character sleeps within 10 ft. of it, the curse begins. From that point, use the Whisper of Vel'Nathis activity (on the item or actor) each long rest to trigger the DC 14 WIS save.

The Vel'Nathis actor is not placed on a map — he exists only inside the shard. Use his biography tab for full GM reference during play.

🔑 GM-Only Content: All pages in this journal are set to GM visibility only. Players will not see secrets, plot hooks, or the Compulsion Table unless you share them explicitly.

👥 Vel'Nathis

Tier B • Tiny Fiend (Demon) • CR 4 • 1,100 XP • Bound within the Shard of Vel'Nathis

@UUID[Compendium.runeforgestudio-velnathis.actors.Actor.0a6794b8af35c266]{⚔️ Open Statblock}

📚 Description

Vel'Nathis is a tiny imp — barely two feet tall when standing — with matte black leathery skin that absorbs light much like the shard he inhabits. His face is angular and precise, with small curved ram horns and a wide thin-lipped mouth that smiles more than any expression should. His eyes are completely white, without iris or pupil, and never blink. His bat-like wings are always folded close to his body. His tail ends in a fine barbed point that curls slowly when he is thinking.

He dresses in nothing, needs nothing, and is immaculate. When he appears in dreams, he is always sitting. He is never standing. He is never moving. He is simply there.

⚔️ Statblock Summary
StatValueStatValue
AC13 (natural armor)HP38 (7d6+7)
Speed20 ft., fly 40 ft.*CR4 (1,100 XP)
STR6 (−2)DEX18 (+4)
CON12 (+1)INT16 (+3)
WIS14 (+2)CHA16 (+3)
SavesDEX +6, WIS +5, CHA +5Prof. Bonus+3
SkillsDeception +7, Insight +6, Persuasion +7, Arcana +5SensesDarkvision 120 ft., Telepathy 60 ft.*
ImmunitiesPoison, Psychic; Charmed, PoisonedResistancesCold, Fire, B/P/S (nonmagical)
LanguagesCommon, Abyssal, Infernal, Deep SpeechAlignmentNeutral Evil

* Movement and telepathy apply only within dream form / while sleeping targets are within 10 ft. of the shard. Vel'Nathis cannot physically leave the shard.

⚖️ Features

Bound to the Shard (Passive). Vel'Nathis cannot physically manifest or leave the Shard of Vel'Nathis. He exists as a bound intelligence within the crystal. He can perceive everything within 60 ft. of the shard and communicate telepathically with sleeping creatures within 10 ft.

Dream Walker (Passive). Vel'Nathis appears in the dreams of those he targets as a trusted figure from their memory. The face is always slightly wrong around the eyes. Characters with a passive Insight of 16+ may notice on a successful WIS save.

Whisper of Vel'Nathis (Special, GM-triggered). Each night a sleeping creature is within 10 ft. of the shard, Vel'Nathis forces a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature follows one of his demands (roll 1d10 on the Compulsion Table). Three consecutive successes drives him into dormancy until the shard changes hands.

⚔️ Combat (Dream Form Only)

Sting. Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 1d4+4 piercing + 2d4 psychic. The target must make a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or be Poisoned for 1 minute.

Note: Vel'Nathis uses the Sting only if he manifests in full dream confrontation (result 10 of the Compulsion Table and the player refuses to engage). This is not a typical encounter — it is a consequence, and a rare one.

🎯 Motivation

Freedom from the shard — specifically, to be carried to a location where the binding ritual can be unmade by proximity to raw planar energy. He has narrowed the viable locations to three and is currently steering the most promising carrier toward the nearest one.

🔒 GM Notes & Escalation Track

Role: Bound manipulator and long-term campaign villain operating entirely through psychological influence and proxy actions.

Vel'Nathis never appears physically — he is a voice, a presence, and a face borrowed from memory. Play him as unhurried and precise. He does not want the characters dead. Dead characters cannot carry the shard anywhere useful.

SessionsPhaseBehavior
1–3The FriendHelpful spirit guide. Low-stakes compulsions (1–3). Never reveals his nature.
4–6The ShiftFace wrong in new ways. Higher-stakes demands (4–6). Hints at real agenda.
7+The OfferDirect bargaining. Results 7–9. Reveals one of the three unbinding sites.
EndgameThe AudienceResult 10. Full confrontation. Banishment or imprisonment required.

The most unsettling thing he can do is agree with the party completely and then explain why that is exactly what he needs.

🤫 Secrets

Vel'Nathis knows the location of the three viable unbinding sites and will not reveal any of them — not even in a bargain. He knows the exact ritual Sorath Keln used to bind him and has spent 140 years refining a counter. The counter requires the shard to be physically present at a planar node AND for someone with free will to speak three specific words — words he has been slowly weaving into the compulsion-driven behavior of his carriers for decades.

🪝 Plot Hooks
  1. The character carrying the shard begins to recognize that the figure in their dreams knows things they have never told anyone — specific memories from childhood. Vel'Nathis has been reading them since the first night.
  2. A previous carrier of the shard is found — alive, fully functional, and completely loyal to an agenda they cannot explain. They are steering a merchant guild toward a ruined location in the mountains. They do not know why.
  3. Vel'Nathis offers the character a genuine gift — accurate information about something they desperately need — with no apparent strings attached. He is telling the truth. The gift is real. The string is that accepting it makes the character more likely to comply the next time.

💎 Shard of Vel'Nathis

Wondrous Item (Loot), Very Rare — No Attunement Required

@UUID[Compendium.runeforgestudio-velnathis.items.Item.87ca0d734c1be397]{🎖️ Open Item Sheet}

📖 Description

A jagged shard of black crystal the size of a fist, its surface cold and perfectly smooth despite its fractured shape. Light does not reflect from it — it absorbs it. In darkness, the shard seems to emit no glow, yet those nearby sometimes glimpse a faint violet shimmer at its core, visible only from the corner of the eye.

The shard radiates no detectable aura under normal divination. It does not register as magical until a true seeing or detect evil and good spell is cast within 10 feet of it.

☠️ Curse — Whisper of Vel'Nathis

Any creature that carries this shard on their person, or keeps it within 10 feet while sleeping, becomes subject to the attentions of the bound demon Vel'Nathis.

Each night during a long rest, trigger the Whisper of Vel'Nathis activity on the item sheet. The sleeping creature must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or be compelled to follow one of Vel'Nathis's demands — roll 1d10 on the Vel'Nathis Compulsion Table.

On a success, the character is aware of a presence but resists. On three consecutive successes, Vel'Nathis withdraws until the shard changes hands.

🔧 Mechanics
PropertyValue
Item TypeLoot (non-equippable, proximity triggers curse)
RarityVery Rare
AttunementNot required — proximity during sleep is sufficient
Detect MagicDoes NOT detect under detect magic
Detect Evil & GoodDetects as fiendish within 10 ft.
SaveDC 14 Wisdom, triggered each long rest
Uses1/dawn (resets each morning — tracks nightly use)
Curse SuppressionDispel evil and good — 24 hours only
Permanent RemovalBanishment or imprisonment cast on the shard
DestructionCannot be destroyed by nonmagical means
Price8,000 gp (if somehow identified and sold)
Weight1 lb.
🔗 Foundry Workflow
  1. Drop the Shard item into the world as treasure. The item starts as unidentified.
  2. When a character sleeps within 10 ft., enable the CURSE: Vel'Nathis Active Effect on the item (currently disabled by default).
  3. Each long rest, click the Whisper of Vel'Nathis activity on the item sheet to trigger the DC 14 WIS save roll.
  4. On failure, open the Vel'Nathis Compulsion Table and roll 1d10.
  5. Narrate the result as a dream sequence.
💡 GM Tip: Do not identify the item immediately. Let it sit as a "Black Crystal Shard" for one or two sessions before the curse triggers. The contrast between "this seems like treasure" and "something is wrong" is the whole setup.

🎲 Vel'Nathis Compulsion Table (1d10)

Roll when a creature fails the DC 14 Wisdom saving throw. The compulsion feels like a personal decision — the character believes they thought of it themselves. They must follow through before their next long rest, or suffer the listed consequence.

@UUID[Compendium.runeforgestudio-velnathis.tables.RollTable.f73cb47f5efda22f]{🎲️ Open & Roll Compulsion Table}

GM Note: The compulsions should not feel obviously evil. A good player will try to fulfil the letter of the compulsion in a way that causes minimum damage — allow this. The point is the erosion of trust and the accumulation of small wrongs over time. Vel'Nathis is patient.
🔄 Compulsion Results
d10CompulsionIf Ignored
1Isolation. Spend at least 2 hours alone today, away from all companions, without explaining why.Disadvantage on all CHA checks until next long rest.
2Misdirection. Tell at least one deliberate lie to a companion today. It need not be significant, but it must be false.1d4 psychic damage each time you attempt to tell a companion the truth until next long rest.
3Acquisition. Take something that does not belong to you today. It need not be valuable. The act is the point.Disadvantage on WIS saves until next long rest.
4Undermining. Subtly discourage a companion from pursuing a goal they care about. Plant doubt, not conflict.-2 to all CHA ability checks until next long rest.
5Concealment. Hide something from your companions today — an object, a piece of information, or an event.Restless sleep: no benefit from your next long rest.
6Escalation. In the next conflict or tense situation, make it worse rather than better. Choose the aggressive option.Vel'Nathis visits twice the next night — two WIS saves required.
7Offering. Leave a small valuable (worth at least 5 gp) somewhere hidden in the environment, given to no one.Disadvantage on all ability checks until next long rest.
8Confession. Share a secret you have never told anyone. It must be true. If you have no secret, invent one and believe it.Vel'Nathis leaves a psychic brand: -1 penalty to WIS saves for the next 7 days.
9Exhaustion. Push yourself beyond your limits today. Accept the most dangerous task, forgo a short rest, or volunteer for the most punishing role. You may not explain why.Gain 1 level of Exhaustion immediately upon waking.
10Audience. Vel'Nathis appears as himself — a small black imp, sitting across a fire, speaking in his own voice, making no pretense. He has one specific request. You must listen to at least one full sentence.If you refuse to listen: 2 levels of Exhaustion + no long rest benefit. If you listen but refuse: no penalty — but he will remember.
📈 Escalation Guidance
Total FailuresGM Guidance
1–4Weight rolls toward 1–3. Low stakes. The party may not notice anything is wrong yet.
5–8Weight toward 4–6. Relationships begin to crack. Other party members notice behavioral changes.
9–12Weight toward 7–9. Waking hallucinations begin. Vel'Nathis speaks briefly during alert moments.
13+Result 10 becomes available. The character is deeply compromised. Only greater restoration + banishment can break the connection without permanent mental damage.

pages.name: Lore & Story — The Making of the Shard

text.content:
📖 Lore & Story
🔝 The Origin of the Shard

Vel'Nathis was not always bound. He was a demon of middling rank who made the mistake of attempting to possess a diviner named Sorath Keln during a negotiation neither party had intended to survive.

Sorath survived. She spent the next eleven years reducing Vel'Nathis from a being to a voice, the voice to a whisper, and the whisper to a presence sealed inside a shard of obsidian she had grown in a circle of silver and salt over three years of patient preparation. She gave the shard to a museum in a coastal city that no longer exists. The museum burned during a siege forty years after her death. The shard did not.

Vel'Nathis remembers everything. He remembers Sorath's face. He remembers the eleven years. He is not angry — anger would be too simple. He is patient. He has been inside the shard for a hundred and forty years and he has gotten quite good at dreaming alongside other people.

🔗 The Chain of Carriers

The shard has passed through at least fourteen known carriers since the museum fire. Vel'Nathis keeps a meticulous mental record of each one. Most were useful for between three months and two years before they either succeeded in carrying him toward a useful location, died for unrelated reasons, or became too unstable to direct reliably.

The previous carrier — a merchant's assistant in a river city — made it as far as a known planar convergence site before succumbing to the compulsions entirely and walking off a dock. The shard was recovered by salvagers. It is currently somewhere between "found treasure" and "interesting curiosity."

Vel'Nathis does not consider this a failure. The merchant's assistant made it forty miles further than the carrier before them.

🏠 The Three Unbinding Sites

Vel'Nathis has identified three locations where the shard could be brought to undo Sorath's binding. He will not reveal any of them under any circumstances — not even in exchange for a plausible promise of freedom. He has learned that promises do not travel well between the dreaming and the waking world.

GMs may define these sites to fit their campaign. Suggestions:

  • A ruined temple at a planar convergence point, currently occupied by a faction with their own agenda
  • A mountaintop where the Astral Plane bleeds through during certain astronomical events
  • A submerged vault built by Sorath Keln herself, containing her notes and one final failsafe
👥 Sorath Keln's Heir

Sorath Keln's great-grandchild — Maren Keln, a middle-aged archivist with no magical ability and a persistent anxiety about a family heirloom they have never been able to locate — is looking for the shard. They do not know what it does. They know only that their great-grandmother's journals describe it as something that must never be allowed to reach "the places between places."

Maren can be a source of exposition, a complication, or a tragic figure who arrives just after the shard has moved on. They are gentle, meticulous, and completely out of their depth. They will not give up.

Vel'Nathis is aware of Maren. He is carefully steering events to ensure that if Maren finds the current carrier, it is after the carrier has already served their purpose.
